top_profane.text:
I'm copying and pasting the review I used at Home Depot, because that is where I bought this compactor. However, I spent a lot of time on this review, and I love buying stuff on Amazon too, so I decided to also put my review here. The exact model I have is the black 15" Elite model, but I'm sure this review applies to just about any of their 15" elite models.

My unit arrived slightly defective. The hold feature doesn't work. So far, I've had a repair guy out three times, and he still has a part on order to try to fix it. Fortunately, the compactor works in normal mode, so this hasn't prevented me from using it. (One other switch broke within the first week, but the repair man was able to fix this on his second visit. So it was only out of commission for about a week.)

The good news is that Broan has been very nice and helpful, and has paid for all of the service visits. So, you can have confidence in their 1-year warranty, and if you get unlucky like me at least you don't have to pay for repairs. With any luck, yours will simply work right out of the box!

Because of these warranty issues I've had to deal with, I can't give this a 5-star review. If it arrived in perfect working order, I would have been happy to give it 5-stars.

But I can still confidently give it 4-stars. I love this compactor. I'll list the positives, and then address some of the negatives I've read about it in other reviews:

Positives:

Excellent horse power. This unit crushes glass bottles with ease. In fact, it is so powerful that I decided to stop crushing my glass to a pulp. Now, I use the first glass bottle "popping" as the sign that my trash is full, and I take it out to the can. By doing so, this has been a great indication of when it has compacted enough for my tastes. (I haven't even come close to overworking this thing).

It looks stylish. I love the black color, and it matches the rest of my appliances. As a free standing unit, it looks great and gets the job done.

Excellent capacity. We can fit three days worth of garbage in this thing, including gallon milk jugs, formula containers, etc. (I have two very small kids. We were going through a full kitchen sized bag every day.) I was having trouble fitting all of the trash small kids can generate in a week (diapers, formula containers, milk containers, etc. etc.) in the single curbside receptacle. Now it is very easy. Two of these bags per week is about our new average. That is amazing. I think we are getting about a 4:1 compression ratio, which I think is great.

Addressing some unfounded criticism:

This unit, freestanding, does indeed tip forward. BUT, once you install the inexpensive front kick plate ($15), this problem goes away. The user manual makes it painfully clear that for a freestanding installation you MUST use this kick plate. This trash compactor has been rock solid ever since I installed that kick plate. I can't get it to tip forward very easily even when I push down on it. Yes, it was very annoying to have to order a second part that I feel should have come with the unit, but I'm not going to knock off any stars over it. Now that I've added this item, I'm over any annoyance I had from needing to place a second order. (I did actually order this kick plate from Amazon, as it turns out.)

Trash bags are expensive. Yes, they are. BUT, the generic compactor bags that are sold at Wal-Mart work just fine, if you stretch them just a bit. Oh sure, the company warns you against doing so, but seriously, it is a plastic bag. For about a quarter of the cost, I'm happy to make do with an off-brand bag. Their official bags are great, and easy to use. I just wish they were more affordable. However, the workaround is so easy, I can't really consider this a legitimate downside. I've even considered using Glad Forceflex bags, which I'm sure would work.

Not enough compacting power? One review said that stepping on trash would work just as well. This isn't true at all. I certainly can't crush wine bottles just by stepping on them, but this trash compactor laughs at glass bottles. I have to empty the trash as soon as I hear glass breaking, or the compactor would turn them back into the sand from which they came. PLENTY of horsepower. It was very difficult for me to fit all of our garbage into one trash can every week, but now it is easy. (And before I had this unit, I was OF COURSE standing on the trash can smashing it down with my body weight. No way I could do as good of a job, not to mention how silly I looked.)

Legitimate complaints: It really isn't as convenient to throw stuff away as with a normal kitchen trash can. You have to open the door, pull out the drawer, and make sure your kids aren't going to tackle you when you are bent over. (Happened to me once, I scraped my arm.) Also, there is no way I want my kids throwing stuff away in here until they get older.

However, my wife and I worked around this minor inconvenience very easily by buying a small trash can (bathroom sized) and placing it nearby. Our daughter knows that this is "her" trash can, and as it fills up we can throw the little bag inside the compactor. This solution actually worked so well that even we tend to use the little can out of convenience. Then you get the best of both worlds, and you can contain organic items in a smaller bag to be compacted later. Works great, and now that we are doing so I don't even consider the compactor to be a hassle. The usefulness more than overcomes any potential inconvenience caused by needing to open a door and slide out a bin.

Oh, and if I haven't mentioned it yet: this thing is FUN. Maybe it is because I'm a guy, but smashing things is kind of satisfying. You feel like your kitchen is that much cleaner. And my little daughter loves watching it go. She runs into the kitchen every time I hit the button to watch the light change from red to green (and she laughs if something pops).

I recommend buying one if you need to smash trash. Like I said, I would easily have given it 5-stars if I wasn't dealing with warranty issues.
top_profane.title: Glad I Bought It

top_profane.text:
I have over the last 20 years owned 4 trash compactors, one Whirlpool and two Sears models, and they they might have been made by Whirlpool anyhow. The compactor is built in under our center isle. When our last Sears model broke, I wanted to see what was available that might be higher quality and perhaps higher capacity. I was not unhappy with the three models I had before, and the pickins are slim in this category. But I found the Broan and after reading the reviews, decided to take the plunge. Here is what I found:

1. You must have a garbage disposal in you sink to deal with organic trash. The house had one when we bought it, and honestly, it is one of the wondrous inventions of the 21st century! All organic trash goes down the drain, so that you are only throwing away paper and plastic essentially, and should never have an issue with odors, although the Broan seems to have an effective odor control feature. Don't really know because out garbage never has a chance to smell.
2. The capacity of this compactor is somewhere near twice the capacity of the other models. This means two things. 1. Fewer trips to the outside garbage can. Fewer bags used. Because of the generous capacity, the unit does not really need or seem to be compacting unless a tremendous amount of trash is in it. We empty the bag once a week because we have once a week garbage pick-up. Honestly, there are weeks when I could go another week because of the excess capacity, but the wife says out is goes! We are a family of 4 or 5 people, by the way, and I think we generate an above average amount of garbage.
3. The front mounted release door( behind the front cabinet door- more on that later) is a breeze compared to the side release of the Sears. The bag goes in easy and comes out just the same. The plastic lined paper bags of the Sears unit was a real pain to fit into the drawer, and just as hard when really full to get out. Also, those paper bags were really difficult to seal, where the plastic Broan bags are not.
4. Relativly quite when operating, and the compact hold on schedule feature is really nice.

Now the complaint. You must swing open the front door, and at least 90 degrees and pull out the drawer to put trash in the container. This is a two step process if you do not want to keep the door open 90 degrees and the drawer sicking out into the kitchen all day to throw away track. The Sears unit, which was IMHO a better design. The "door" was the facia of the garbage drawer, and had a no hands step lever which levered the draw open 6 to 8 inches so you could throw away trash. Simple, easy , and so much less intrusive then an open door and a manally pulled out drawer!

Over all, I put up with it because of the other advantages, and it really seems to be a well built, solid machine.
top_profane.title: Best I've owned...one small complaint

top_profane.text: As my first trash compactor ever I think this is perfect.  I've reduced my weekly trash output roughly 1/3, by compacting the trash.  Instead of paying more to the City for an additional trash bin, I'm able to fit all our weekly trash in the single bin.  After using it a month I wish it had more horsepower, so I could further reduce the trash, but its adequate for my purposes:  smashes most everything into neat compact rectangles that now fit nicely within the trash bin that goes to the curb.  I even have some extra space to start working away on the clutter.  I use it with the Whirlpool trash compactor bags which have held up nicely.  Its not too noisey and seems to be made to withstand our frequent open/shut routine of throwing things away almost hourly.  I would recommend this for "normal" household trash use, which is all I use it for.  Based on my experience thus far  I'm not sure it could handle "shop" trash .
top_profane.title: LOVE IT!

top_profane.text:
I am writing due to the piece of crap Cabrio we purchased exactly 4 years ago this week:
This was the review I wrote after having this piece of crap one month:
I bought the Cabrio on 10/9/06 and by 10/23/06 it was not working properly. I contacted Whirlpool on 11/9/06 and they informed me that they needed to send a repairman out. It has several problems. The load is out of balance (UL) or I get an error "F51" every single time I try to wash a load. My husband and I are the only ones in our household so the machine is not abused (By teenagers etc). The repairman was at our house for 3 1/2 hours on 11/14/06. He ordered a wiring harness, a computer board and a censor for this 1 MONTH OLD MACHINE. I called Whirlpool in frustation simply asking them to take this LEMON back. They offered nothing and they were quite rude in the process. They said my only option was to have my machine repaired....and so the circle begins.
Our last set was a Maytag and we had the set (still are using the dryer) for over 12 years. Boy how we regret going to Whirlpool. Please take my advice and SAVE YOUR MONEY. I would hate for anyone to go through what Whirlpool has put us through.
BTW...my BRAND NEW machine is sitting idle until the repairman can come out IN A WEEK (for another 3 1/2 hours) to put in these parts that he HOPES will work.
UPDATE:
It is now exactly 4 years later and the only reason I suffer with this machine is due to the fact we PAID SOOOO MUCH MONEY FOR IT!!
The front is now rusted out!!! YES, RUSTED OUT!! And, it takes me about a day to wash ONE LOAD of Laundry...I get an "F1" (I believe it should be FU) about 4 times at the beginning of a load and then when it goes to rinse I get an F1 again approximately 3 times!! Sometimes I have to start from scratch with an entirely different setting.
The only satisfaction I've gotten over the past 4 years is writing horrible reviews on your product and making sure everyone I talk to about laundry (and it's a lot more times than any normal person should have to talk about laundry!!) how they should NEVER spend a dime on a whirlpool!
This is an absolute piece of crap and I cannot believe you have never done a thing to rectify this situation!!
So sad that you could have and should have taken care of this FOUR YEARS Ago!
When my husband calls your useless 800 number he just gets sent around in a big circle and then is told you are CLOSED.
I will never buy another Whirl pool product as long as I live!!
Please do something about this piece of crap taking up space in my laundry room and BEEPING at me ALL DAY LONG!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
top_profane.title: DON'T DO IT!!!!!!!!

top_profane.text: Stay away from this fridge.  With all of the problems I have had, I will NEVER buy Whirpool family of products ever again.  Read reviews, and issues before even considering this brand.  It only lasted 2 years before problems started happening.  First to go was the ice maker.  I called a repairman out as the warranty had of course just ended.  He said he could replace it for min. ~$300, but said he couldn't guarantee it would last.  Then the deli drawer plastic hinge broke. That's just crappy design, plastic hinges?! Crap.  Lastly is the constant leaking.  The freezer leaks about 12-16 ozs of water all at once every few days.  I researched this, and it is common problem, basically the condensing unit's drain freezes, causing excess water from condensation to not drain into the drain pan where it should evaporate, but buildup in the bottom of the freezer which creates an ice damn, and then spills on your floor.  Piece of crap, stay away

I paid about 1 dollar per bag.  I have a trash compactor and prior to finding these bags, I used regular ole' trash bags.  Well, big mistake!  Regular bags run down the side of the trash compactor causing more mess and worst of all, I would get my hands all disgusting and dirty from having to readjust the garbage bags at least 1-2x per day.  The bag would rarely stay in place when I turn the compactor on.  After buying real Trash Compactor bags, I never have to get my hands dirty, the bag stays in place and does not rip or tear.  I only have to replace the bag once a week.  That works out to be a dollar a week for headache- free trash disposal!
top_profane.title: LOVE LOVE

I didn't buy this on Amazon, i got it at Lowe's
We had the same Emerson Front load wash for about 10 years, it died. Bought this just to use while having our house remodeled, then we will splurge on a sweet high end set. Have had it about a year. I started noticing a "mildew" smell on my washed closed. Like that smell you have when you leave a shirt in the wash wet? for a day or two? that sour, nasty smell? i absolutely ABHOR this smell. I told the wife about it, and suspected it might be the detergent she switched to. No. We tried that definitely this crap can machine.

so the issues are similar to what others have said i and i just want to add another bad review for this POS.

1. Washer sensing is moronic, Ive watched it do full cycles on several different settings. Crap can design water never fully submerges all clothes. I understand HE man, but how in the HELL does clothes become "cleaned" if it never comes in contact with the detergent\water? it doesn't. What kind of QA does freaking Maytag have?!?!?
2. So you do the deep-wash, fine finally fills the thing with enough water, but the freaking agitator things doesn't do anything! The water just floats while this thing does a 180 waltz back and forth. Old top down washers have a big "screw" like agitator that actually grabs a hold of the freaking clothes. this thing cant do that.

I might was well tell the old lady to fill the bathtub up and throw all the damn clothes in there. Then ill get her one of those Old timey corrugated metal agitator thingies the old maids in the 1800s used with both hands. it would do a better job than crapbox. I feel so cheated for buying this thing....
DO NOT BUY
top_profane.title: Complete dog crap

top_profane.text:
OK, here goes a little back story...
I never knew these existed until about 2 weeks ago when I was researching dishwashers for my new place. Since there wasn't one installed and no place for one, I was looking at finding/ buying matching cabinets (if I could find them), replacing the counter tops, plumbing, electrical, the whole deal... then I'd be ready to buy the $500 dishwasher. If you're in a crappy apartment without one, buddy this thing is perfect for you. It fits right where that stupid dish rack goes and you can take it with you to the next crappy place!

Out of the box...
Packaging was incredible! Plenty of protection by the manufacturer but Amazon added more in an outer pack box. Zero damage, everything was there, directions decent enough to read and follow. The unit had a funky smell when I first set it up, but it went away after the first use.

Rubber hits the road...
Setup was easy, screw on the faucet adapter (makes your faucet piss like a race horse), slap on the hoses and your off to the aforementioned races.Ran a pretty good mix of dishes for the maiden voyage, I don't have pretty matching dishes like the pictures, so it's a free for all. Obviously its kinda small so there is definitely a method to arranging your dishes to get max dishes in and max clean. This unit only has a spray arm on bottom so keep that in mind when you load... this isn't your mother's Kenmore.
Controls are ultra simple, no words just icons for the different cycles, so make sure to read up about each.For purpose of basline comparison, I've done all 5 washes so far in the normal mode.
The unit is super quiet when it's running, I can't tell if its going until I get right up next to it, or it dumps/ brings in water... that I can certainly hear. Heats the water really well as it needs to because there is no heating element like most modern dishwashers (again, not mom's Kenmore). Here's a fun party trick... after you shut down the faucet to pull off the hoses, if you forget to hit the red button to relieve the back pressure... you'll wash your whole kitchen the quick n wet way... so don't forget.

OK OK enough BS, how'd it do....
...not too frickin shabby at all! My dishes haven't been that clean since I last had a dishwasher, but I'm not the dish whisperer either. For giggles I ran things that had been sitting for a while, they did fine. I also threw in a greasy pan that barely fit along with a few other things. The pan flopped over during wash and was blocked on the bottom by a plate, so it didn't do so well. Make sure to load everything in the proper place, vertically and snugly and you won't have a problem. Also, don't forget the jet-dry... its there for a reason.

Soooo, am I a happy knuckle-dragger?
YES! I LOVE THIS THING! Awesome idea, awesome price, awesome performance! The only question is... how long will it last?? Time will tell, but it looks to be a real simple design and well put together so I expect it will do well. If it doesn't... I'll update this review with my revenge list!
Come on... lazy dudes UNITE! (OK girls, you can come too). Get one of these and clear out that nasty-ass sink!
top_profane.title: Awwwwww Yeeeaah!

top_profane.text:
I already reviewed this. It works really great!. You must have a 5 gal bucket though for it to work its best. You can wash 2 smaller things at same time or one medium size thing. I have never used it for anything real large. I am guessing it has its limits for size of things and amounts of things. It is just in a 5 gal bucket it works real good for its size. This would be nice to wash a few things while camping or real crappy things you don't want to throw in your nice washing machine. So it is ideal for pre-washing real crappy things before putting into the real washing machine.

It has its purposes and occasionally I really need this thing for crappy things.

10/06/15 update. I have been using this thing on my back porch with a 5 gallon bucket. It works best with a talk 5 gallons bucket. This model comes without a handle. I already had a sturdy wooden mop handle with metal screw in tip and it worked perfect with this Mobile Washer. I can wash a pair of jeans and shirt or several shirts in the 5 gallon bucket. I have big electric washer and dryer but I love this thing to wash really too dirty things I don't want to wash in my regular machine. I have dogs and they sometimes mess on their doggy blankets. I can wash in the 5 gallon bucket instead of in the big washer. I also prewash things that are exceedingly dirty in the 5 gallon bucket with this thing before putting things in my regular washer. I don't know why people say this thing doesn't to a good hob washing because it does.

I don't have to plunger it very much. I will put clothes in bucket with detergent and plung about 10 times then let it rest and come back later and plug it 10 more times, real quick. Then I can rinse out the wash and hang it out to dry or throw in the dryer.

All I can say is with little plunging effort it has gotten some pretty dirty things really clean for me. It has solved a lot of special laundry problems for me. It is such a simple basic thing that really works.
top_profane.title: Mobile Washer REALLY WORKS WELL.

top_profane.text:
Do not buy any appliances that are Samsung or Sears Kenmore because Kenmore slaps their name on defective Samsung appliances.

(Please, stay with me, I know this isn't a Sears Website)

I am writing a review on Amazon concerning the most expensive refrigerator Sears sold four years ago. It was made by Samsung. And I would like to forewarn other consumers that Samsung is not on the up and up regularly. If you call Samsung they deny making the refrigerator I purchased. In the four years we have had this piece of expensive crap it has broken down six times. Six times translates into many repairman visits per breakdown incident because they do not know how to fix this refrigerator. It has a plethora of design flaws. Sears knows that they sell defective products and obviously do not care. You must buy the extended warranties or you will be laying out huge amounts of cash for the repairmen to come out only to ATTEMPT to solve the puzzle of what is wrong with this piece of crap. You pay by the hour for repairmen if you do not have the warranty. You pay for the parts if you do not have a warranty. You pay with multiple days of inconvenience and many people have to take days off from work to be home and present when the repairmen come.

It's winter so I have been putting my food outside. The refrigerator, this time, has been out of commission for over a month. I have lost far more food than the $250 check Sears sent to cover the cost of lost food because the weather has been fluctuating. It's 55 degrees outside right now and promises the same for tomorrow. A good refrigeration temp is 38 degrees. The repairmen are here right now for their sixth visit for this one breakdown incident.

And. resentfully, I pay $260 each year for the extended warranty.

How insane to have to put out that kind of money yearly for a new appliance!!!!

The first year we had this thing the repairman made his first visit when we had the fridge for three months.

Two years ago the one repairman was so confounded that he went and re-took the Sears repairman training class for refrigeration. He did this so he could be instructed on the repair of my refrigerator model. The Kenmore Pro. The class instructor has no knowledge of this refrigerator and so could not instruct the repairman as to how to fix it's many problems.

Sears sells a product they know their repairmen cannot fix.

Sears sells a product that the manufacturer denies making.

So, essentially, it's a shell game. Isn't that nice?

I have gone through Thanksgiving and now Christmas without a refrigerator.

The new parts arrived here a week ago before Christmas and I let Sears know the parts were here and they made no attempt to make a new attempt to fix this piece of crap before Christmas.

How they do not care about their customers!!!

I always read reviews before making a purchase here on Amazon. Our toaster died a week ago and in the toaster reviews one person wrote something very wise. She wrote advice to just buy the cheapest toaster because there are bad reviews for all of the toasters regardless of the pricing.

For me, that pretty well sums it up about most appliances.

I've spoken with so many friends and associates about this problem and the general consensus is that it is ubiquitous.

I have a friend who bought a new fridge four years ago. So many repairs later...they just bought another refrigerator to replace the four year old fridge because they got no where with the manufacturer or the seller.

The same friend has replaced her furnace twice in seven years.

There are so many accounts and I'm sure any one of you readers could come up with your own library of accounts known to you.

Our government allows these manufacturers/sellers to get away with ripping off the consumer. It's time for a consumer revolt.

Really. Really.

I will never, ever buy so much as shoelace from Sears ever again.

We sold our other home four years ago and moved to this new home. We left our old refrigerator at the old home and the people who bought our old home love the refrigerator that came with it...it's almost 30 years old and works like a charm.
top_profane.title: Consumer Beware!!!!!!!!!

Apparently the Cuisinart uses early K crap technology technology. This maker is to much simpler to use and maintain, Compared to my $170 K crap one that only lasted about a year of light use. top_rant.text:
I JUST purchased the 'NEW FOR 2016, 10 lb capacity model #PAN310DG'.
I will truly try to keep this short as I would like to save others from my mistake.

Okay, I took a chance due to all of the reviews (most of which did not apply to my model but still I took a chance).
I did my research in 1 day (yep, not much time).... but unless you rec'd what I did and furthered your search ----- Well, you would NEVER had learned what I did!
Additionally, I will be including only a limited number of photos here (in hopes that (as usual, photos get top priority in the review) as I really would rather not waste any more of my time on something that I will be returning the same day it arrived.)
Think what you may, but this item really has issues! And I can almost promise that no one wants to deal with them.
And I reiterate, ONLY DUE to the number of reviews did I purchase this VS The Laundry Alternative 'anything else' !!

One other item, regarding the pics:
One is how I ended up having to feed water into the unit AND the carpet samples to keep it from creeping.... the other is the FURTHERED water that the 'drainage hose' accumulated whilst writing this review (hopefully you can see the 'sort of blue water in the hose' ---- WHEN IT HAD ALREADY DRAINED COMPLETELY THE FIRST TIME!!! Yes, it would further accumulate due to no 'spinning dry effect' but do you really want to deal with a potential mess tomorrow morning or whenever!?!?!!!!!

PROS:
It TRULY IS A NICE BIG washing tub!!! (best I can do here....sorry Panda)
Beyond that, nothing else regarding this machine impresses me NOR is worth my (or your) time&effort.

CONS:
Oh my, where to start?!?
I rent, so of course I am worried about 'leakage' from anywhere.
Once it arrived, I cancelled the rest of my day to test this out.
1) Instructions are from a foreign country with attempted English. I'm okay with that, but really? Thankfully I had read enough of the reviews and instructions to make it work.

2) The water input and output hoses are a joke!
My water input hose had leaks in 2 places causing water to disperse everwhere when attempting to use it. They are SOOO VERY THIN, not to mention that the input water hose wouldn't even 'attach' to a newer Kohler faucet properly. Seeing that, I avoided the intake hose and just basically used the 'green bowl that you will see in the pic' in order to fill it up initially. I wanted to run this without ANY CLOTHES inside in order to see if it leaked anywhere.

3) There is a reason that the instructions say to 'ADD soap and agitate for ONLY 1-2 minutes'. While I added no soap, with water filled ONLY 1/4 of the way ---- the entire unit started CREEPING ALONG on my bathroom counter ALMOST to the end of it once I set the timer. Thankfully, I caught that moment in time before a disaster happened. Vibration & CREEPING is huge on this item with only water!
a) With only water inside the tub and the timer set to approx 10 min, drips of water
began leaking from the 'top lift-up cover' down the machine and under the unit & onto the counter.
Thankfully, I was there to see this and caught the issue before something may have
caused an electrical issue due to the motor being on the bottom of the unit. At that point,
I learned that yes, only 1-2 min of agitation would be best for laundry soap added.
b) Additonally, I still needed to use some vinyl coated carpet samples that I had and hoped that
might alleviate the CREEPING. They seemed to help.

4) I decided that with perhaps some weight of clothes and detergent AND YES it did....
However, I did not take 15 minutes to watch to see if either the carpet samples or the machine continued to 'creep' towards the edge side of my counter. At this point, I was 'SO completely 'uninthralled' over the entire thing' and switched off the timer. I DO NOT HAVE TIME TO 'literally watch' my washing machine!!! Do you?? So, that's when I went to google and youtube and found 'what might HOPEFULLY make me happier'. On youtube, watch 'thomfoz' for Almost Everything The Laundry Alternative! While I had read about the 3 year warr from 'TLA' ---- the lack of reviews here on amazon prevented my initial instincts to purchase one of their 'portable/countertop washing machines'.

5) One more 'con'...... you'd BETTER HAVE A NICE BIG SINK (or tub) to allow the drainage tube to drain into!
After all of the above efforts, I decided to wash a couple tshirts and potholders w/soap. One of the tshirts bled some blue and for the first time ---- I could see that the UPWARD HOOKED-ON DRAINAGE TUBE (it works on gravity to drain, but) was at or more than 3/4 filled with water! I get it --- upward facing and gravity ------but did I really want to WORRY about this tube spilling over!?!?!??? ......AND this is why I am taking the time to write this review!!! I was BEYOND DONE with every BAD issue this machine had!!

I'm sure there were more things that were wrong regarding the Panda unit.... but I've no time left to 'even take the time here'....... it really just sorta sucks!!! The one (other than warr) thing that TLA offers beyond a 3 year warr, is that their SuperPoP has a PUMP to 'UPSUCK' the drainage water into a kitchen sink!!! It comes with a hook which or which may not work with your countertop ----- but NO MORE (issue #5 abv) hoping that you don't spill all over the counter and floor the drainage water!
NO GRAVITY DRAINAGE INVOLVED!!!!
AND, while the hook may not work on your countertop, you sure will not be attempting to control the flow of the drainage water (while you attempt to place it into your sink without spilling!!!!!!!!!) You can just lay it into your sink if need be!!!
This SURE BEATS attempting to keep a lot of water from flowing all over the counter depending on the angle that you are holding the drainage tube BEFORE EVEN MAKING IT STEADY ENUF TO FLOW INTO YOUR SINK ONLY!!!! There is absolutely no CONTROL of the drainage tube on the Panda....do your best of grab a roll of paper towels!!
Beyond that, the SuperPop HAS A RINSE CYCLE that ONLY ALLOWS WATER OUT !!!
Should you EVER UNHOOK the upward facing drainage tube on the Panda downwards (or the hook breaks)....... you'll be left with a mess of water everywhere ---- let alone a poss lawsuit from your downstairs neighbor for all of the damage. Whether it be your home or the tenant below ---- do you really want to deal with the mess that this might cause?!???

My unit is going back defective ----- 2 holes in the intake hose.....
My next attempt will be 'something, anything!' from TLA!!!
With a 3 year warr, and a pump to PUSH WATER UPWARDS into the sink ---- I would rather have that fail than this crappy thing! This Panda unit causes too much concern, too much mess, and too much wasted time otherwise!!
I hope this might help others find the 'right purchase' their first time.
PS: have not purchased TLA Superpop yet, but after this review posts ---- I WILL BE!!
I just don't see how it can be as much as an issue as this unit. Esp, because it has that pump to extract water upwards into a sink VS a loose canon of a hose that pressurizes water and CANNOT WAIT TO SPOUT it's contents out!!

Again.... I REITERATE!!!! ------The Laundry Alternative has a 3 year warranty on ALL OF THEIR PRODUCTS. The Panda ONLY has a 6 month warranty. See 'thomfoz' on youtube for other options to your needs!!! I am hoping that his 'love of reviewing washing machines' meets my final choice needs and perhaps yours instead of this product :)

PPS: I had taken the chance after all of my research to purchase the TLA Nina Spin Dryer..... it just arrived. While I do not know about the TLA SuperPop ---- the Nina Spin Dryer has HUGE RUBBER FEET (almost suction cup like). I will be testing this tonight to wring out my still soapy Panda Wash (my bad, too much detergent apparently)...... and I don't know if the SuperPop will include the same rubber feet.... but I am thrilled with the TLA Nina Spin Dryer already!!! Something to prevent creeeping along any floors or countertops!!!!! Hoping it does just that and that the SuperPop comes with the same xlnt rubber feet once it arrives!!!
Good luck to you all.... I DO truly hope this helps your purchase happiness!!!

(PPPS: I try to avoid reviews due to the cost going up when folks love what they get.... but I just couldn't NOT review this Panda and sleep well tonight :)
top_rant.title: Live & Learn the BAD way....or RETURN this for The Laundry Alternative SuperPop (even cheaper)...

top_rant.text:
Ok, I debated buying this product for several wks. its a new product, not a lot of ratings, but good ones for the most part...so then price was on the high side...well, what to do? What got me to buy has NOT been disappointing!

1. Fridge doesn't have those crappy wire rails, where everything slips around, and can storage I have zero use for! Nope it has the wonderful REAL racks! Yes! My large and medium size water bottles of Propel stay all nice and neatly in the door and even in a shelf I have some racked in! I love it! And I can put my meds inside, and my snacks, and if I want pretty much everything for sandwiches, left overs etc. I was determined to NOT have those crappy wire things for the door, and those stupid can holders, who in world wants to be stuck having what they do NOT want or use, when dealing with limited space to begin with? PROBLEM IS SOLVED!

2. The SEPARATE FREEZER that actually FREEZES! This thing is HUGE!! You REALLY can put an entire bag of ice in there! I however use a gallon zip lock bag, put my ice in there from the downstairs fridge ice maker, and am good to go! This is being used in our master bed room, in the sitting area right by the recliner. I even have my Ben and Jerry's in the freezer with no issues. Well its not "frozen Hard" but its frozen and perfectly eatable! :) If you need a freezer you gotta get this fridge freezer! It's really a miniature regular fridge like you'd have anywhere, just small. But for a mini fridge, well its HUGE in SPACE!! I am not exaggerating! This is the absolute best mini fridge I have ever owned and I have owned several.

3. The light inside is perfect, just like a reg fridge!

4. The doors have the quiet sound you expect when closing a fridge that has a good seal and is made of quality!

5. It is QUIET! Yes, there are times I do hear it click on, but as sensitive as I am to sounds, (and I am) it has never bothered me. Its about 9 ft from the flat screen tv on the wall, and I do have surround sound in the bedroom, so I can't say that the sound is not somewhat drowned out when the tv is on. I want to point that out because I really do understand the issues some of us have with sensory (sound), and I should make sure to note this for those of you who also have same. That said, Its not something that I've ever noticed often. When I don't have the tv on or music playing, I am not disrupted when I am going to sleep tho. BUT that may not be the same for all people. I do think it would be extremely RARE for this fridge to be a issue in regard to sound tho. And if you don't have it in a bedroom you won't even need to be concerned, its truly quite over all by far! Just now and then I do hear the compressor. Rare tho!!

6. Shelves do adjust, and are quality and well. made!! I cannot imagine having better space inside this fridge by any other fridge. You simply move as you desire! And the door space is plenty spacious as I already mentioned! I have zero issues with fitting whatever I want in here. I have to say, I don't have it full, and probably never will, I have it up stairs due to a spinal injury and my wife and I decided I should do this so I can be more independent and not fighting the stairs when its night time and I want ice added to my propel, or I want ice cream to snack on when watching tv. and then some meds that need to be refridged can be right up stairs end of night, Im not going back down stairs to do these things. if we end up later going to a one story home, I will still want this in my man cave ha! Perfect!

I have only had it about a month. It was easy to set up, but they said to keep it seated for I believe it was like 8 hrs or something (in the instructions) BEFORE plugging in for use. I sat it up and left it a full 24 hrs and then plugged in. I have no real idea why, but I figured better safe than sry!

Warranty, Set Up, an General Info!
There was ONE 3 inch scratch on it. And I had to laugh. It was done Right where the line was for the plastic thin wrapping layer they put on the ENTIRE FRIDGE!! THE ONLY, ONE and ONLY place it could have even gotten scratched and somehow, it managed to do it! Its not super noticeable, and sure not something worth sending it back for! But I do want to note that this did happen. Also that thin sheer plastic wrapping stuff, ugh ugh ugh for taking it off! BUT again as bad as that is to deal with, worse would be the damage that could have been without it! So you take your time and just work it off! They really packed this thing WELL with VERY High Quality Smooth THICK FOAM, in several fitted pieces, and SMARTLY done!! They did not go cheap on how its shipped!!

I kept the box for about the 30 days I assume was reasonable to test. If you don't have "box" it came in they will NOT accept the return if needed! I think you'd have some legal grounds here, and man is it a PAIN to keep that HUGE box!! But after a few wks I threw it out today. It was taking up way too much space in our over filled garage and this Fridge is amazing! I seriously doubt its going to need anything anytime soon!! But be sure to hang on to your box for a few wks just to be fairly sure!

I do hope this review helps others, decide if this is a fit for them. No I am not in anyway related to anyone with this company. Like most of you, I was searching high and low for the RIGHT fridge with the shelving a door racks that I really wanted, and was willing to pay for. For this price, YEP its actually worth every penny!! If this lasts me a few yrs. I am fine! If it last longer (and I do hope so), then its a Awesome bargain! If it only lasts lets say 2-3 yrs I like it so much so far, I'd buy it again! Absolutely! Now Im going to head up stairs, and have me some Ben and Jerrys with a nice Netfiix show or something! Yes, loving this fridge!! PS I forgot to mention the separate door on the freezer, A MUST and you'll soon find out why! I accidentally left the fridge open the other night, was a middle of the night water run, and I just didn't push close enough. Wife found it open and told me about it. Man was I glad that the freezer was sealed! My IceCream would have totaled out! Oops! ...not to mention the bag of ice that would have melted! Hey, if you have the money to get this, and the space, do it! I can't imagine any issues with this quality! And then enjoy!!
top_rant.title: YES FINALLY THE PERFECT FRIDGE!

top_rant.text:
UPDATE: Thursday, December 12th, 2013. I've been using my Speed Queen ATE50 washer Dryer combo for over a year now and all I can report is trouble free use, clean clothes and dependability. I started having slight mold issues but Borax reduced it. Using bleach to rinse out the washer once a month cut the mold issue way down. Finally I have it set so only hot water goes into the machine so no matter what the waters settings say hot water is being used all the time. Lucky for me I don't have dress clothes and delicates. My underwear are already gray in color because that's the only color I buy. I found using nothing but HOT WATER in all cycles really reduces the mold issue to almost zero when used with the other steps I outline. Yes I know my solutions are not for most people. Goodness knows I am for sure not most people. But after just over a years use my Speed Queen still works like a champ. No issues at all none nada. Oh and by the way the dryer is still working like gang busters too! I'll take my Speed Queen over the junk it replaced any day.

REVIEW: To start I had a stacked pure crap Frigidaire Affinity washer and dryer that did not EVEN survive 2 full years of lite use. Next I had a Whirlpool stacked laundry center that conserved water like a champ but did not get my clothes clean. The attached Whirlpool dryer never got things completely dry. The ever pleasant Whirlpool technician explained how Whirlpool re-engineered the dryer to use a new heat sensor which prevented the dryer from completely drying my clothes. Another case of new sub-standard useless technology that does not work replacing old proven technology that worked well every time!

First let me say I brought my ATE50 Speed Queen washer \ dryer from an authorized dealer and it was properly installed by experts. Proper installation might cost more but it often improves performance and service life in the long run. Also yes this Speed Queen washer and dryer costs more than most washer dryer combinations. The Speed Queen payoff is years of clean clothes without near constant problems, breakdowns and drama you risk suffering with most of these badly engineered high priced garbage front loading washing machines. Remember that the United States Congress COMMANDED these new Washers to save water at the expense of never properly cleaning anything. Like most things Congress "Requires" these new High Efficiency washers work only to save water for the environment at the expense of consumers and taxpayer! Rich guys in the U. S. Congress could care less that regular people walk around in moldy rags thanks to their new Washing Machine regulations. They have people to clean for them so washing and other common chores are not their concern. So ordinary consumers must buy Energy Star washer that works for you. Beware that Congress has passed additional water saving standards that command washers to save more water at your expense so if you are thinking about buying a new washer dryer get it now while the more consumer friendly Energy Star Eco-Nazi washer water saving standards are in place.

I brought the Speed Queen Model ATE50 Washer Dryer Laundry Center combo and it is AWESOME! First the washer is good size at 3.3 cubic feet. The washer uses just 13.9 gallons of water per cycle but, it actually gets the clothes CLEAN! There is also a button that lets you add an extra Rinse and Spin to the standard cycle using more water to get rid of any left over detergent. Also if you want it is possible to add water to any load via the detergent drawer, the washer won't drain extra water out.

I don't mind a GREEN Washer so long as it gets the clothes CLEAN! Choosing the wash cycle is simple and easy. All the basics are there regular, delicate, spin only, rinse and spin. The washer does not have all the timer do-dads and other bells and whistles but, the old style clock work timer mechanism dryer and very basic manual electronic hybrid washer controls are much less likely to break with everyday use. Speed Queen means quality because this washer brand is often found in high traffic Laundromats where machines take lots of abuse. The washer is extremely quiet. The spin cycle does have a very slight jet plane whistle but no where near as bad as most models. I had to listen carefully to detect the washer was in spin mode. Best of all the washer rinses detergent out of washed articles completely. I had no problems with unbalanced loads, excess wobble, shaking or walking. (A washing machine that moves or otherwise shifts in location as a result of clothes spinning, agitation or other normal functions is said to WALK!)

I had no issues with mold mildew or washer funk just leave the washer door ajar for a few hours after each load. The controls are located low between the washer and dryer for easy access. The washer and dryer controls are clearly marked with bold writing. The washer dryer controls are logically and intelligently laid out so there use should be intuitive. The Speed Queen ATE50 spin cycle was a real surprise. The washer when spinning did not seem loud at all. I used to think if a washer made the devil's own noise while spinning your items would be more likely to come out with most of the water removed, ready for drying. This Speed Queen made very little noise and when spinning did not appear to be going too fast. I was prepared to have sopping wet clothes to dry when the washers final spin cycle completed. I was shocked when I opened the door and the clothes were just barely damp. The Speed Queen ATE50 spin cycle is both quiet and effective.

The upper Speed Queen ATE50 Electric dryer drum is positively cavernous and able to take a large load with ease. The dryer heats up quickly making quick work of dryer loads. The Speed Queen is much more quiet than both my prior Frigidaire Affinity and stacked Whirlpool Laundry Center. My only concern with the Speed Queen is the washer's almost paper thin flimsy rubber door gasket. You can't afford to make a mistake leaving coins, keys or other things in this Speed Queen ATE50 washer. Even the smallest item left in the Speed Queen ATE50 washer will rip its cheap thin rubber gasket to shreds. The ATE 50 washers door gasket is almost paper thin, with a cheap paper thin feel that makes it appear easy to tear or damage. My lame old Frigidaire Affinity washer was pure crap but it had a solid strong almost indestructible door gasket that never tore leaving no excuse for the worthless cheap paper thin door gasket used by this Speed Queen Washer.

It is a shame that an otherwise highest quality Speed Queen washer has a cheap dime store washer gasket. Another bad thing about the Speed Queen ATE50 dryer is its cheap plastic window in the dryer door. top_rant.title: THE BEST WASHER DRYER COMBINATION BUT IT COULD BE MUCH BETTER!

top_rant.text:
I absolutely hate this washer. I've owned it for 18 months now and every time I use it I regret the purchase. Here are some of the reasons I hate it.

1. It uses waaaaay too little water. When I bought it the salesman told me that the machine fills to the top of the clothes plus 1 gallon. That is simply not true. The clothes stick out above the top of the water by 9 inches. The water level is slightly adjustable by the soil level setting but this only changes the level by an inch or two. I have experimented for hours to try to increase the water level - the machine is determined to keep the level low probably so that they can get their highest-level energy star rating. Now you may think that it is a good thing for it to use too little water but when you see how harsh it is on your clothes (item 2 below) and how wrinkled the clothes get (item 4 below) you will change your mind.

2. It is extremely harsh on clothes. When I bought it the salesman told me that the machine is more gentle than older style agitator machines. That is completely untrue. This washer pills clothes visibly with every single wash. Brand new towels appear old and worn after a few washes. Here is why it is so harsh. The clothes throughout the load are wet but they are not submerged in water. Only the bottom of the load is submerged in water. The washer tries to pull the clothes up and down into and out of the water at the bottom. It does so by twisting and spinning them, similar to the way clothes are twisted when you wring them out. This is harmful for clothes. When clothes are submerged in water (as in older washers) the water acts as a cushion against rubbing. The water is moved by the agitator and the clothes glide around in it. The higher the water-to-clothes ratio the more gentle it is on the clothes. The lower the water-to-clothes ratio, the more tightly the clothes are packed and the more abrasive the rubbing is. This washer is the most extreme case of a low water-to-clothes ratio because the clothes aren't even submerged at all. They are wet and rubbing hard against one another. To make matters worse the wringing action, which is done very tightly, is very harmful for anything but the sturdiest of fabrics. Imagine taking a wet shirt and twisting it as hard as you can from end to end in a wringing action. This is what clothes endure during each load.

3. There is no way to wash delicates in this washer. For all the reasons mentioned in 2, there is no setting on this washer that is acceptable for delicate clothes. The handwash cycle on this washer is much harsher than the normal cycle was on my old washer. None of my work clothes can tolerate it.

4. Clothes come out incredibly wrinkled, twisted, and knotted. As mentioned in 2, this washer needs to twist and knot items to move them in and out of the water. When the load is done they remain this way. Bras are all knotted together in one giant mess that takes forever to undo. I have to first shake items out to untwist them and then dry them on a higher heat setting than I would otherwise use to get the wrinkling out. Use of the lower-speed spin cycle doesn't really help much because the problem is largely due to the washing portion of the cycle. The high-speed spin cycle only sets the twisting in even harder.

5. There is no way to change washer settings after the load has started. How many times have you started a load and then decided, "oops I'd rather use handwash" or "I'd rather use high-speed spin after all". Well forget about changing your mind on this washer. This washer goes through an extensive calibration cycle at the beginning of each load to figure out how much water to use. The calculations it does during this time are based on the cycle settings such as soil level, load type, etc. It can't let you change settings once the calibration cycle has started because its calculations will get messed up. Maytag says that all you need to do is press cancel and restart but what makes this so incredibly annoying is that cancel drains all the water from the washer. It often happens to me that I want to change settings after the washer is partly full of water plus soap. To make a setting change I have to throw away the entire load of water and soap. You wouldn't believe the amount of water I've wasted because of needing to press cancel.

6. The soak cycle wastes water and soap because once the soak cycle is complete there is no way to continue using the same water to complete a wash. It gives you no option but to drain the entire tub after the soak cycle. Then when you want to wash you have to refill it again and add more soap.

7. The calibration cycle takes a long time. As mentioned in 5, this washer goes through an extensive calibration cycle at the beginning of each load to figure out how much water to use. It takes about 5 minutes and makes the cycle take that much longer with the only benefit that it is deciding on the "optimal" amount of water to use. I would much rather have the level manually selectable and the cycle 5 minutes shorter.

8. The electronic smarts are very limiting and annoying. The salesman told me "this washer is so great that it won't allow you to damage your delicates". What he meant is that the washer limits your options depending on what cycle you have selected. So for example, if you have the washer set to the handwash agitation, it won't let you use high-speed spin or let you use warm water. Or if you use the soak cycle it wont let you use hot water. Or if you use the soak cycle there is no way to get delicate agitation. There is no way to override their setting limits. How ridiculous! I know better than some engineer at Maytag what options are appropriate for me. To think that they advertise this as a feature?!?

9. The lid locks. Sometimes when washing delicates I like to be able to keep the lid open during agitation so that I can assist with special pieces. This washer won't allow any cycle to continue with the lid open although I did find a way to bypass the lid lock function.

10. There is only one positive thing about this washer. I like the glass lid because it lets me see how harshly the washer is twisting my clothes.

Some reviewers here have commented that maybe I did not properly follow the instructions about how to distribute clothes within the washer. That is not true. I read and follow the instructions perfectly - it doesn't help.

The first day after I got this washer I tried to return it but the store wouldn't take it back. I called Maytag and begged them to tell me how to increase the water level. I tried to pay a service person to increase the water level. I kept being told there is absolutely no way to increase the water level.

I am an electrical engineer and I got so frustrated with Maytag that I spent about 8 hours working out a way to make the water level on this washer adjustable. It requires adding a potentiometer (variable resistor) to the control board that is accessible from the top of the washer. The potentiometer adds an offset of water to the level. Right now I have it set to add an extra 10 inches of water so that the clothes are completely but just barely submerged. It makes a huge difference in wear and tear on clothes and makes the handwash cycle usable. The washer is now just barely bearable. If you want to use my technique for adjusting water level, email me at sueonline@gmail.com and I'd be more than happy to share the method with you (for free). I even have pictures showing the control board modification.

They come across my review and email me. I hear the same story over and over again from them. They bought this washer and hate it fiercely. They are tired of clothes coming out still dirty and are tired of their fabrics getting destroyed. They want help increasing the water level. As an added note to the issue you mention I am now noticing (having never had this occur before with my washer of 15 years) that some of my clothes are coming out with what appear to be brown BURN marks. I first noticed a pillowcase with a brown line on it - thinking it was dirt - I scratched my fingernail gently on it the fabric and the fabric disintigated/broke apart!
